skill.name: design-taste-frontend
skill.description: Guides AI coding tools to create polished React and Next.js interfaces with stronger layout, motion, typography, and state design choices.
skill.summary: Builds distinctive frontend interfaces with practical design and performance guardrails.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown inline code, design terms, and prompt guidance. The skill is a prompt-only frontend design guide with no executable code or confirmed malicious behavior. The remaining low risk is that it may guide generated apps to use an external placeholder image URL and to inspect package.json before adding dependencies.

low_findings.title: External Placeholder URL Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill recommends using a picsum.photos placeholder URL for generated examples. This is not credential exfiltration, but generated applications may contact a third-party image host if the guidance is used unchanged.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is direct evidence of an external URL in the prompt guidance. Risk is limited because the skill does not execute network requests itself and no sensitive data is sent.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Dependency Check Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the AI agent to check package.json before importing third-party libraries. This is legitimate project inspection, but it does require read access to a local project manifest when applied.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The package.json inspection instruction is explicit and narrow. It does not request secrets, broad filesystem traversal, or file modification.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution. Review found prose, inline code examples, package names, CSS snippets, and React terms, not executable shell or Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown guidance and the flagged backtick content is not executed by the skill. No scripts, command runners, or shell interpolation are present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match design terminology and Markdown text. No hashing, encryption, authentication, or cryptographic API usage was found in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain design labels, CSS values, and frontend guidance. There is no cryptographic implementation or use of weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts map to frontend phrases such as global state, grid layout, loading states, fake numbers, and pre-flight checks. No system inventory, host probing, or environment discovery behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms are used in UI design and React architecture context. The skill does not request operating system, network, account, or secret discovery.

content.value_statement: AI-generated interfaces often look generic and miss important interaction states. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concrete frontend design rules for stronger layouts, motion, and polish.
actual_capabilities: Defines baseline design variance, motion intensity, and visual density settings for generated interfaces.
Guides React and Next.js component structure, including Server Component and Client Component boundaries.
Provides Tailwind CSS layout, typography, color, spacing, and responsive design rules.
Requires loading, empty, error, hover, active, and motion states in generated UI work.
Adds performance guidance for animations, GPU-friendly transforms, z-index use, and scrolling effects.
Lists specific high-end interaction patterns for navigation, grids, cards, galleries, and typography.
limitations: It is a prompt-only skill and does not include runnable code, tests, or automation scripts.
It may over-constrain projects that already have a mature design system or strict brand rules.
Some suggested animation libraries require existing project dependencies or installation by the user.
The external placeholder image guidance can add a third-party asset dependency to generated examples.

use_cases.title: Improve AI-Generated Product Screens
use_cases.description: Generate less generic dashboards, settings pages, onboarding flows, and feature views with stronger visual hierarchy.

use_cases.title: Create Motion-Rich React Components
use_cases.description: Ask for interactive UI components with safer animation boundaries, responsive fallbacks, and polished state handling.

use_cases.title: Review Frontend Output Quality
use_cases.description: Use the final checklist to identify weak typography, generic layouts, missing states, and risky animation choices.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Product Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a polished first screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a React product page using this skill. Include responsive layout, strong typography, clear states, and a distinctive visual direction.

prompt_templates.title: Redesign an Existing Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request using an existing component or page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign this frontend screen with the skill rules. Keep the same product goals, but improve layout, hierarchy, color, motion, and mobile behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Interactive Dashboard
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for dense application UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Next.js dashboard using the skill. Include loading, empty, error, and active states, with motion isolated in client components.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Generated Interface
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this frontend implementation against the skill checklist. Identify generic design patterns, missing states, performance risks, and responsive layout issues.

output_examples.input: Build a SaaS analytics dashboard with charts and recent activity.
output_examples.output: A dashboard plan with asymmetric layout, compact data sections, monospace metrics, loading states, empty states, and isolated animated widgets.

output_examples.input: Make this landing page feel less generic.
output_examples.output: A redesign direction with stronger typography, fewer card boxes, one controlled accent color, relevant imagery, and responsive spacing rules.

output_examples.input: Add animation to a feature grid without hurting performance.
output_examples.output: A motion approach that uses transform and opacity, avoids layout animation costs, and isolates continuous effects in small client components.

best_practices: Use the skill after you define the product type, target user, and design constraints.
Check the existing package.json before requesting libraries such as Framer Motion, GSAP, or Three.js.
Ask for screenshots or visual verification when applying the guidance to a real app.
anti_patterns: Do not apply the rules blindly to apps with strict brand guidelines or existing design systems.
Do not add perpetual animation to dense operational tools unless it supports user comprehension.
Do not use external placeholder images in production without replacing them with approved assets.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown instruction file that guides AI-generated frontend design and implementation.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it limited to React?
faq.answer: It defaults to React or Next.js, but many layout and design principles can guide other frontend stacks.

faq.question: Does it require Tailwind CSS?
faq.answer: The skill strongly prefers Tailwind CSS and includes Tailwind-specific guidance for generated code.

faq.question: Can it replace a designer?
faq.answer: No. It improves AI output quality, but human review is still needed for brand fit and usability.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The only confirmed concern is guidance that can introduce an external placeholder image URL into generated examples.
